name: signed_lift_symbol_bindings_v1
purpose: Bind symbolic loop letters to provisional base-graph meanings before signed-lift evaluation exists.

convention.inverse_notation: s^-1 means the same edge traversed in reverse orientation

q0.meaning: chosen base vertex of G15 for symbolic bridge loops

q1.meaning: intermediate vertex on the first twist path and first square edge target

q2.meaning: common endpoint of the two-path comparison

q3.meaning: intermediate vertex on the second twist path

q4.meaning: third vertex in the provisional square cycle

x.meaning: oriented edge from q0 to q1 for global_return

a.meaning: oriented edge from q0 to q1 for twist path 1

b.meaning: oriented edge from q1 to q2 for twist path 1

c.meaning: oriented edge from q0 to q3 for twist path 2

d.meaning: oriented edge from q3 to q2 for twist path 2

s1.meaning: oriented edge from q0 to q1 for the square cycle

s2.meaning: oriented edge from q1 to q2 for the square cycle

s3.meaning: oriented edge from q2 to q4 for the square cycle

s4.meaning: oriented edge from q4 to q0 for the square cycle

notes: This file does not define the signed lift yet.
It fixes a reading convention for symbolic bridge loops on the base-graph side.
global_return is explicit at the base-graph level: q0 --x--> q1, then back by x^-1.
global_twist is explicit as two paths from q0 to q2: [a,b] and [c,d].
global_square is explicit as a dedicated 4-step cycle: [s1,s2,s3,s4].
